$comment: Conformance fixtures + declared verdicts. run_conformance.py runs the same reference validator against each. 'pass' fixtures (generic-vault, minimal-vault) are stored complete deployments. 'fail' fixtures declare a `tamper`: the runner copies the generic vault to a tempdir, applies that single tamper, validates, and discards it — so no duplicated trees and no secret-shaped strings live in the repo. expect_reasons are matched against the package validator's (procheiron.validate) actual error strings.

fixtures.name: generic-vault (Meridian Atelier)
fixtures.note: FULL-CONTENT second deployment: same parameterized Core + reference impl, different config/profile/records/authority. The real 'second deployment passes conformance' proof.

fixtures.name: minimal-vault (5-file v0.1)
fixtures.note: smallest passing tree — validated by the 5-file adopter's own minimal validator, not the full reference.

fixtures.name: neg: active record without independent review
neg: prose supersession without structured supersedes
expect_reasons: prose supersession claim without structured supersedes

fixtures.name: neg: deployment literal welded into Core doc
neg: tilde-home path welded into Core doc
neg: audit event silently edited (broken hash chain)
